claims.claim_text: Sunflower grown after a nutrient-exhaustive rice crop risked nutrient depletion, but adequate nitrogen applied to the preceding rice prevented the succeeding sunflower from being severely deprived.
claims.evidence_text: The application of 120 kg N /ha to rice recouped its removal by the crop from the soil. Therefore, the succeeding sunflower was not severely deprived of the nutrient absorption.

value.raw_value_text: The application of 120 kg N /ha to rice recouped its removal by the crop from the soil

context.input_system: rice-sunflower sequence with N applied to rice

provenance.source_title:
Influence of conjuctive use of organic and inorganic source of nutrients in rice (Oryza sativa) on crop growth, yield components, yield and soil fertility in rice- sunflower (Helianthus annuus) sequence
							| Indian Journal of Agronomy
